title: Project Operations: New and Existing Projects
description: Create a SceneMax project from the Projects menu, then reopen an existing project and confirm the active workspace.
subject: Project operations
clipFocus: Use File > Projects > New Project, name hello_dragon_tutorial, then use File > Projects to open an existing project.

title: Hello World
description: Create a folder and script file, then write a Hello World scene that loads the bundled dragon, plays fly, and rotates left.
subject: Hello World
clipFocus: Create the dragon_scene folder, add a hello_world script, save the runnable main file, and preview the default dragon flying while turning left.

title: Package and Run a Saved Game Executable
description: Package the saved dragon project as a Windows executable, open the output folder, and launch the game.
subject: Package and run executable
clipFocus: Use Package Program, select the Windows target, build the saved game, open the generated folder, and run dragon_scene.exe.

title: Printing Text
description: Print simple text on the screen, style it, position it, update it through a named channel, and clear it when it is no longer needed.
subject: Printing Text
clipFocus: Use sys.print to show text, place it with pos, style it with color, size, and font, then reuse the sys channel to update or delete the text.

title: Playing 3D Animations
description: Play a bundled animation on a 3D model and control its speed.
subject: Playing 3D Animations
clipFocus: Use s.Dance to play the Dance animation on a Sinbad model, then use a speed value to make the animation slower or faster.

title: Quick Start
description: Declare an imported Effekseer effect, choose where it appears, and play it during the game.
subject: Quick Start
clipFocus: Explain the basic Effekseer runtime flow: declare the effect, choose a position or target, then play it.

title: Declaring An Effekseer Effect
description: Give an imported Effekseer asset a SceneMax variable name so later commands can use it.
subject: Declaring An Effekseer Effect
clipFocus: Explain how effect declaration names an imported Effekseer asset for later play, attach, hide, show, and delete commands.

title: Playing An Effect
description: Use the play command to start an Effekseer effect in the scene.
subject: Playing An Effect
clipFocus: Explain the play command and why effects are normally triggered at a gameplay moment.

title: Play At A Fixed Position: Playing An Effect
description: Play an effect at exact world coordinates when the effect belongs to a place, not a moving object.
subject: Play At A Fixed Position
clipFocus: Explain fixed-position effect playback with coordinates and when to use it.

title: Play At Another Entity: Playing An Effect
description: Play an effect at another object so the effect appears where that object is right now.
subject: Play At Another Entity
clipFocus: Explain playing an effect at a model or another entity instead of manual coordinates.

title: Start From A Predefined Placement: Playing An Effect
description: Set an effect's starting placement when declaring it, then play it later at a target.
subject: Start From A Predefined Placement
clipFocus: Explain initial placement on declaration and why it helps reuse an effect with a prepared offset or rotation.

title: Runtime Attributes
description: Pass runtime values such as playback speed or dynamic inputs when an effect starts.
subject: Runtime Attributes
clipFocus: Explain attr lists, playback speed, and dynamic input values for effects authored to react to them.

title: Looping An Effect
description: Keep an effect running until you hide, delete, or replace it.
subject: Looping An Effect
clipFocus: Explain when to use loop and how a looping effect differs from a one-shot burst.

title: Example: Multiple Runtime Inputs: Looping An Effect
description: Combine loop with runtime attributes when an authored effect supports dynamic inputs.
subject: Example: Multiple Runtime Inputs
clipFocus: Explain multiple runtime inputs in a looped effect and when beginners should use them.

title: Aiming The Effect Before Play
description: Turn a directional effect toward its target before playing it.
subject: Aiming The Effect Before Play
clipFocus: Explain look at for directional effects such as lasers, cones, and missiles.

title: Face Another Object: Aiming The Effect Before Play
description: Use look at with another object so the effect faces that object.
subject: Face Another Object
clipFocus: Explain aiming an effect at an entity rather than manually calculating rotation.

title: Load With Initial Rotation: Aiming The Effect Before Play
description: Give a directional effect an initial rotation when the imported asset faces the wrong way.
subject: Load With Initial Rotation
clipFocus: Explain initial rotation on an effect declaration and when it fixes asset orientation.

title: Attaching Effects To Other Game Objects
description: Attach an effect to an object so it moves together with that object.
subject: Attaching Effects To Other Game Objects
clipFocus: Explain attachment and why it is different from playing once at the current position.

title: Attach To A Model: Attaching Effects To Other Game Objects
description: Attach an effect to a whole model so the effect follows the model.
subject: Attach To A Model
clipFocus: Explain model-level attachment for a character aura or object marker.

title: Attach To A Joint: Attaching Effects To Other Game Objects
description: Attach an effect to a skeleton joint such as a hand for weapon or magic effects.
subject: Attach To A Joint
clipFocus: Explain joint attachment with quoted joint names and why it is useful for hands and weapons.

title: Attach With Offset: Attaching Effects To Other Game Objects
description: Attach an effect with a local offset so it follows the parent from the right spot.
subject: Attach With Offset
clipFocus: Explain attachment offsets and why they help place effects relative to the parent.

title: Detach From Parent: Attaching Effects To Other Game Objects
description: Detach an effect so it stops following its parent and becomes independent again.
subject: Detach From Parent
clipFocus: Explain detach from parent and when an attached effect should become independent.

title: Show, Hide, And Delete
description: Control an effect object's visibility and cleanup after it has been created.
subject: Show, Hide, And Delete
clipFocus: Explain hide, show, and delete as the effect object lifecycle.

title: Hide: Show, Hide, And Delete
description: Hide an effect when you want it invisible but still available for later.
subject: Hide
clipFocus: Explain hide and how it differs from delete.

title: Show: Show, Hide, And Delete
description: Show a previously hidden effect so it becomes visible again.
subject: Show
clipFocus: Explain show as the command that makes a hidden effect visible again.

title: Delete: Show, Hide, And Delete
description: Delete an effect when it is finished and should be removed from the scene.
subject: Delete
clipFocus: Explain delete as cleanup for an Effekseer runtime effect.

title: Full Example
description: Combine declaration, aiming, attributes, attachment, visibility, and cleanup in one effect flow.
subject: Full Example
clipFocus: Explain a complete beginner-friendly effect sequence used in a combat situation.

title: Packaging And Scene Changes
description: SceneMax packages declared Effekseer assets and cleans them up when scenes change.
subject: Packaging And Scene Changes
clipFocus: Explain automatic packaging and scene cleanup for declared Effekseer effects.
